name: Rainbow Pride
description: The original 6-stripe LGBTQ+ pride flag — a Gilbert Baker design, the most widely recognised symbol of the movement.

name: Progress Pride
description: Daniel Quasar's 2018 redesign — adds a chevron of black, brown, light blue, pink, and white to centre marginalised groups within the rainbow.

name: Intersex-Inclusive Progress Pride
description: Valentino Vecchietti's 2021 update to the Progress flag — adds a purple ring on a yellow field to also include intersex people.

name: Queer
description: A pink-blue-lavender gradient used as a broad banner for queer identities outside or across the more specific labels.

name: Gay men (mlm)
description: Five-stripe blue-green-white-pink-purple — men loving men.

name: Lesbian
description: Seven-stripe sunset (Emily Gwen, 2018) — women loving women.

name: Bisexual
description: Magenta-purple-blue, Michael Page 1998 — attraction to two or more genders.

name: Pansexual
description: Pink-yellow-blue — attraction to people regardless of gender.

name: Polysexual
description: Pink-green-blue — attraction to many but not all genders.

name: Omnisexual
description: Pink-magenta-black-blue-cyan — attraction to all genders, with gender still factoring into the attraction.

name: Asexual
description: Black-grey-white-purple — little or no sexual attraction.

name: Graysexual
description: Grey-toned ace variant — occasional or low-intensity sexual attraction.

name: Demisexual
description: Black-grey-white-purple with a triangle — sexual attraction only after a deep emotional bond.

name: Aromantic
description: Green-light green-white-grey-black — little or no romantic attraction.

name: Demiromantic
description: Aromantic-style stripes with a demi- triangle — romantic attraction only after a deep emotional bond.

name: Abrosexual
description: Green-white-pink gradient — a sexuality that shifts over time between orientations.

name: Polyamory
description: Blue-red-black with a gold infinity-heart (Red Howell, 2022) — practising or open to multiple consensual loving relationships.

name: Transgender
description: Light blue-pink-white stripes (Monica Helms, 1999) — gender identity differs from sex assigned at birth.

name: Non-binary
description: Yellow-white-purple-black (Kye Rowan, 2014) — gender outside the binary of man/woman.

name: Genderqueer
description: Lavender-white-green (Marilyn Roxie, 2011) — gender identity outside or across binary categories.

name: Genderfluid
description: Pink-white-purple-black-blue — gender that shifts over time.

name: Agender
description: Black-grey-white-green stripes — without a gender, or gender-neutral.

name: Bigender
description: Pink-purple-blue-white-blue-purple-pink — identifying as two genders, simultaneously or alternately.

name: Demigender
description: Grey-yellow demi- pattern — a partial connection to one or more genders.

name: Neutrois
description: White-green-black — a gender-neutral or null gender identity.

name: Intersex
description: Purple ring on yellow (Morgan Carpenter, 2013) — born with sex characteristics outside typical male/female categories.

name: Two-Spirit
description: Rainbow flag with two feathers in a circle — an Indigenous North American umbrella term for gender-variant and LGBTQ+ identities rooted in tribal traditions.
